data.title: Fed holds rates, faces pressure from Trump. July saw disappointing job growth - only 73,000 added. Previous reports revised down by 258,000.
data.description: In July, the Federal Reserve resisted pressure from President Donald Trump and kept interest rates unchanged, marking the first time in over three decades that two board members dissented, advocating for an immediate cut. However, this decision could prove detrimental as recent labor market data revealed signs of weakness. Payroll growth in July was notably below expectations, with only 73,000 jobs added compared to the anticipated 110,000. Moreover, past figures for May and June were significantly revised downward, reducing previously reported job gains by a total of 258,000. The situation is not favorable on the inflation front either, as the Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) price index saw a notable increase. Further details can be found on Benzinga.com.
WH Sr. Adviser Hassett: White House respects the Fed independence.

data.title: Cardlytics grants 454,600 restricted stock units to 2 new employees on July 30, 2025, under Nasdaq Listing Rule 5635(c)(4).
data.description: In Atlanta, Cardlytics, Inc. (NASDAQ: CDLX) revealed that on July 30, 2025, the Compensation Committee of Cardlytics’ Board of Directors awarded a total of 454,600 restricted stock units to two recently recruited employees. These stock units were provided as significant incentives to join Cardlytics, following Nasdaq Listing Rule 5635(c)(4), and were issued under the Cardlytics, Inc. 2022 Inducement Plan (the “2022 Inducement Plan”).

data.title: Asana (NYSE: ASAN) to release Q2 fiscal year 2026 results on September 3, 2025, followed by a webcast at 1:30pm PT.
data.description: In San Francisco, Asana, Inc. (NYSE: ASAN)(LTSE: ASAN), a prominent work management platform integrating human and AI coordination, revealed its plan to unveil its financial outcomes for the second quarter of fiscal year 2026 on Wednesday, September 3, following the closure of U.S. markets. A webcast is set to accompany the release, scheduled for the same day at 1:30 p.m. PT (4:30 p.m. ET) to delve into the financial figures. The event will be available for live streaming and there will be a replay option.

data.title: Trump's policy flip-flop impacts Southern Copper Corp; SCCO stock down 7% last 5 days, 10% last month. Expert warns of repricing refined copper after announcement of 50% import tariff exclusion. Global demand for copper to double due to energy transition, AI investments. Contrarians see opportunity in SCCO stock.
data.description: President Trump's wavering trade policies have had a volatile impact on Southern Copper Corp (SCCO) stock. Initially benefiting from a 50% import tariff on copper, SCCO saw a surge in demand and prices. However, a recent exclusion of refined copper from the tariff caused a sharp decline in SCCO stock, now down 7% over five sessions and 10% in the last month. This policy shift has analysts like Tom Price concerned about market repricing. Despite the recent downturn, the global demand for copper remains high for economic modernization and technological advancements, potentially offering a discounted opportunity for contrarian investors looking towards SCCO stock. The need for copper is expected to double to support global energy transition and other technological developments, offering a sliver of hope for SCCO investors.

data.title: "Energy Monster to go private in merger with Mobile Charging Group Holdings for an equity value of $327 million."
data.description: Smart Share Global Limited, a consumer tech company traded on Nasdaq under EM, known as "Energy Monster," revealed a significant development on Aug. 01, 2025. The company has finalized a definitive Agreement and Plan of Merger with Mobile Charging Group Holdings Limited, Mobile Charging Investment Limited, and Mobile Charging Merger Limited. Per the Merger Agreement, Merger Sub will merge with Energy Monster, making it a wholly-owned subsidiary of MidCo, with an equity value estimated at approximately US$327 million. The acquisition is set to be carried out by a group of investors called the Consortium.

data.title: MAX Power Mining Corp. closes $2.45 million in private placements, with Eric Sprott leading the investments.
data.description: In Vancouver, British Columbia, on August 1, 2025, MAX Power Mining Corp. (CSE: MAXX; OTC: MAXXF; FRANKFURT: 89N) revealed the successful completion of non-brokered private placements of Units, generating C$2,450,000 in total gross proceeds. The leading investor in these Offerings was Eric Sprott. This development follows the Company’s prior announcements on July 15 and July 23, 2025.

data.title: Amgen declares $2.38 per share dividend for Q3 of 2025, to be paid on Sept 12.
data.description: Amgen, headquartered in Thousand Oaks, California, released news on August 1, 2025, declaring a dividend of $2.38 per share for the third quarter of 2025. This dividend is scheduled to be distributed on September 12, 2025, to shareholders of record at the end of business hours.
